Colorado Unemployment Insurance — Benefits Eligibility Policy

Effective: January 1, 2026 | Authority: C.R.S. § 8-73-107

1. Monetary Eligibility

To qualify for unemployment benefits, a claimant must have earned sufficient wages during the base period (the first four of the last five completed calendar quarters before the claim effective date).

1.1 Minimum Wage Requirements

  • Total base period wages must be at least $2,500
  • Wages in the highest-earning quarter must be at least $1,800
  • Total base period wages must be at least 1.25 times the high-quarter wages

2. Weekly Benefit Amount

The weekly benefit amount (WBA) is calculated as 60% of the claimant's average weekly wage during the two highest-earning quarters of the base period, subject to the following caps:

  • Minimum WBA: $25 per week
  • Maximum WBA: $781 per week (effective January 1, 2026)

3. Duration of Benefits

Maximum benefit duration is determined by the number of weeks of qualifying employment in the base period:

  • Minimum duration: 8 weeks
  • Maximum duration: 26 weeks

During periods of high unemployment (state insured unemployment rate above 6%), an additional 13 weeks of Extended Benefits (EB) may be available.

4. Work Search Requirements

Claimants must conduct an active job search each week to remain eligible. Requirements:

  • Minimum 5 employer contacts per week, at least 3 of which must be direct applications
  • Contacts must be recorded in the MyUI+ portal by midnight Sunday of the week claimed
  • Contacts must be for positions the claimant is qualified for and available to accept
  • Refusing a suitable offer of work without good cause results in disqualification for 8 weeks

5. Eligibility Conditions — Weekly Certification

Each week, the claimant must certify all of the following:

  1. Was able to work and available for work
  2. Was actively seeking work as required
  3. Did not refuse suitable work
  4. Reported any earnings from part-time or temporary work (earnings above $25 or 25% of WBA, whichever is greater, are deducted dollar-for-dollar)

6. Disqualifying Reasons for Separation

A claimant is disqualified from receiving benefits if the separation from employment was due to:

  • Voluntary quit without good cause attributable to the employer
  • Discharge for misconduct connected to the work (misconduct defined as deliberate violation of a reasonable employer rule or conduct showing willful disregard for the employer's interests)
  • Fraud in connection with a claim

Disqualification for voluntary quit or misconduct lasts until the claimant has earned at least $1,500 in new employment after the date of disqualification.

7. Expedited Service — Emergency Protocols

Claimants experiencing severe financial hardship may request expedited processing. Expedited service is available when the claimant certifies any of the following:

  • Receipt of an eviction notice or utility shutoff notice within the last 30 days
  • Household income below 50% of the federal poverty level
  • Active medical emergency affecting the claimant or a household member requiring immediate income

Expedited claims must be resolved within 5 business days of the request.

8. Claimant Age Requirement

There is no minimum age requirement for unemployment benefits. Any worker who meets the monetary and non-monetary eligibility criteria may file a claim, including workers under age 18 who have qualifying employment.

9. Enrollment and Initial Filing Window

Claims must be filed within 3 weeks of the first week of unemployment (the week the claimant became unemployed). Late claims may be filed with a showing of good cause; good cause exceptions are granted at the adjudicator's discretion and are not automatic.

10. Benefit Year

Once a claim is established, the claimant has a 52-week benefit year in which to exhaust their available weeks. The benefit year begins on the Sunday of the week the initial claim was filed.
